commands.review_summary:
The branch centralizes inbound media URI parsing, rewrites sandboxed inbound refs to staged `media/inbound` paths, prevents image-tool workspace resolution for media-store URIs, and adds regression tests.

PR surface: Source +46, Tests +113. Total +159 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current-main source shows both the native loader and direct image tool can treat `media://inbound/<id>` as a workspace-relative path, and the linked issue plus PR tests describe the failing paths.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer labeling and the proof gate require maintainer acceptance or added real proof; I did not find a narrow code repair to queue.

target.title: fix(media): resolve inbound media refs consistently
target.body:
## Summary
- Centralize inbound media URI parsing and sandbox path rewriting for managed `media://inbound/<id>` references.
- Hydrate native prompt images from the host media store outside sandbox and from staged `media/inbound/<id>` paths inside sandbox.
- Keep direct image-tool `media://inbound/...` refs out of workspace-relative path resolution when `workspaceDir` is set.

Fixes #87024.
Supersedes #87055; thanks @TurboTheTurtle for the original fix and proof.

## Verification
- `pnpm format:fix src/media/media-reference.ts src/agents/sandbox-media-paths.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts src/media/media-reference.test.ts src/agents/sandbox-media-paths.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/media-reference.test.ts src/agents/sandbox-media-paths.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: inbound Telegram/channel images saved as managed media and referenced as `media://inbound/<id>` now load through native prompt-image hydration, sandboxed native prompt-image hydration, and the direct image understanding tool instead of being treated as workspace-relative `media:/inbound` paths.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout, Node/Vitest runtime, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/media/inbound` files, temporary sandbox workspace with `media/inbound`, and mocked provider image/PDF/generation tool paths.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused Vitest command listed above after committing on top of current `origin/main`.
Evidence after fix: 16 test files and 518 tests passed; branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: host and sandbox managed inbound URI regressions load the real PNG bytes, and direct image-tool refs stay as media-store URIs when a workspace is configured.
What was not tested: live Telegram delivery was not exercised in this PR; the managed media-store filesystem path, sandbox staging path, and direct/native agent image-loading behavior were verified with local files and focused tests.

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>
target.author: steipete
